steps.title: Connect prey type to energy
steps.content: The passage contrasts two diets: non-tool-using otters rely on abundant, energy-poor snails, while tool-using otters can access high-quality mussels and clams. The inference should follow from that energy difference.

steps.title: Reject unsupported otter claims
steps.content:
• Choice A: The text says tool use lets otters process mussels and clams without tooth damage, so this reverses the tooth-damage evidence.
  • Choice B: Tool-using otters gain fitness benefits, so they are not presented as less healthy.
  • Choice D: The passage does not say otters can eat mussels and clams without tools more efficiently.

steps.title: Choose the energy inference
steps.content: If snails provide less energy than mussels and clams, otters limited to snails would need more prey to meet the same energy needs as tool-using otters. The answer is C.
